reasoning: The question requires predicting whether there will be 1 or more fatalities in Azerbaijan recorded by ACLED in the 30-day periods preceding three resolution dates. The given reference value for the past 360 days is 0.500 fatalities per 30-day period (i.e., 6 total fatalities over a year). Given that political violence and border clashes (the primary sources of ACLED-recorded fatalities in Azerbaijan) tend to be clustered rather than evenly distributed, the probability of at least one fatality occurring in a specific 30-day period is generally less than the 39% implied by a Poisson distribution with lambda=0.5. The empirically provided base rate for "any-increase ACLED questions" is 0.225. Without a search tool to check specific incidents during the already-elapsed portion of the first target period (Oct 3 - Oct 26), I am relying primarily on the reference class base rate of ~0.225 for the first resolution date. To adhere to standard forecasting principles and the specific instructions to increase uncertainty backward toward 0.5 for longer horizons, I adjust the probabilities upward for the subsequent dates (0.27 for 2025-11-25 and 0.33 for 2026-01-24).
